refnamediv.refpurpose: configure clipboard sharing and read or write live virtual machine clipboard data

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The VBoxManage clipboard command configures clipboard sharing for a virtual machine and reads or writes live clipboard data for a running virtual machine. The live data commands use standard input and standard output by default, which makes them suitable for shell pipelines.

Clipboard data transfer requires the virtual machine to be running and the shared clipboard mode to allow the requested direction. Use the set-mode subcommand to enable or disable shared clipboard access.

refsect2.title: Specify How to Share the Host OS or Guest OS Clipboard
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard vmname set-mode command specifies how to share the guest or host OS's clipboard with the host system or VM. Valid values are disabled, hosttoguest, guesttohost, and bidirectional. The default value is disabled. See .
This feature requires the &product-name; Guest Additions to be installed in the VM.

refsect2.title: Specify If Files Can Be Transferred Through the Clipboard
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard vmname set-filetransfers command specifies if it is possible to transfer files through the clipboard between the host and VM in the direction(s) configured for the set-mode. Valid values are off and on. The default value is off.
This feature requires the &product-name; Guest Additions to be installed in the VM.

refsect2.title: Copy Host Data to the Virtual Machine Clipboard
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard copy command reads data from host standard input, publishes it to the live virtual machine clipboard, and notifies the guest about the selected format. Use shell pipes or input redirection to copy data from a host file as ordinary clipboard data.

By default, the command waits indefinitely until a matching guest data request is observed. Use --timeout=0 to return immediately after publishing the data. If --timeout is specified with a finite non-zero value, the command waits up to that many milliseconds. The shared clipboard mode must allow host-to-guest transfers.

listitem.para: Specifies the MIME type for the clipboard data. The default is text/plain;charset=utf-8.
Treats the input as raw payload bytes. This option requires an explicit supported MIME type specified with --format; generic application/octet-stream clipboard data is not supported.

Sets the maximum wait time for a matching guest data request, in milliseconds. The default is to wait indefinitely. Specify 0 to return immediately after publishing the data. A timeout stops the wait but does not retract the published clipboard data.
Prints diagnostic information to standard error, including listener setup, published data, received events, ignored requests, guest read requests, and timeout details.

refsect2.para: For example:
refsect2.screen: $ echo "hello from host" | VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" copy $ cat foo.txt | VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" copy --format='text/plain;charset=utf-8'

refsect2.title: Paste Virtual Machine Clipboard Data to the Host
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard paste command reads data from the live clipboard of the virtual machine and writes guest-owned clipboard data to host standard output. By default, it first tries currently available guest data and then waits indefinitely until matching guest clipboard data is available. Use --timeout=0 or --wait=0 to read only currently available guest data. With a finite non-zero wait option, the command waits until matching guest clipboard data is available or the timeout is reached. It does not synthesize a paste keystroke in the guest. Use shell redirection to write the clipboard payload to a host file, for example VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" paste > foo.txt.

If the command would print multiline text to an interactive terminal, it displays a safe preview on standard error and asks for confirmation before writing the clipboard payload to standard output. Redirect or pipe standard output to avoid this prompt.

listitem.para: Requires the clipboard data to have the specified MIME type.
Sets the maximum wait time for matching guest clipboard data, in milliseconds. The default is to wait indefinitely. Specify 0 to read only currently available data.
Deprecated compatibility alias for --timeout=ms.
Prints diagnostic information to standard error.

refsect2.para: For example:
refsect2.screen: $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" paste > foo.txt $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" paste --timeout=10000 > foo.txt

refsect2.title: Offer Host Files or Directories to the Virtual Machine Clipboard
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard transfer offer command publishes one or more host files or directories as a clipboard file transfer using the live set-filetransfers setting and the IClipboardTransfer API. Directory transfers are recursive by default; use -R or --recursive for explicit command-line intent.

The command publishes the transfer and waits for clipboard transfer events such as progress, interaction, completion, cancellation, or failure. Use --timeout=0 to return immediately after publishing the transfer. If --timeout is specified with a finite non-zero value, the command waits up to that many milliseconds. The shared clipboard mode must allow host-to-guest transfers. send is an alias for offer.

listitem.para: Indicates that a directory path is intended to be transferred recursively. Directory clipboard transfers are recursive by default.
Sets the maximum wait time for clipboard transfer completion, in milliseconds. The default is to wait indefinitely. Specify 0 to return immediately after publishing the transfer.
Prints diagnostic information to standard error, including transfer publication and transfer events.

refsect2.para: For example:
refsect2.screen: $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" transfer offer --timeout=0 ./host-file.txt $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" transfer offer -R ./host-directory $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" transfer offer ./host-file.txt ./host-directory

refsect2.title: Receive Virtual Machine Clipboard Files or Directories on the Host
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard transfer receive command waits for or uses a guest-to-host clipboard file transfer and materializes its file tree below the specified host directory. The command uses IClipboardTransferEvent notifications for transfer state updates and responds to supported interaction requests, such as approval, destination, and overwrite requests. The shared clipboard mode must allow guest-to-host transfers. recv is an alias for receive.

listitem.para: Sets the maximum wait time for a guest-to-host clipboard file transfer, in milliseconds. The default is to wait indefinitely. Specify 0 to use only currently available transfers.
Prints diagnostic information to standard error.

refsect2.para: For example:
refsect2.screen: $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" transfer receive ./from-guest $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" transfer receive --timeout=10000 ./from-guest

refsect2.title: List Clipboard File Transfers
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard transfer list command lists clipboard file transfers currently known to the live virtual machine clipboard.

listitem.para: Restricts the listed transfers by direction. The default is any.

refsect2.title: Cancel a Clipboard File Transfer
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard transfer cancel command cancels the clipboard file transfer with the specified transfer ID. Transfer IDs are shown by VBoxManage clipboard transfer list and in verbose transfer event output.

refsect2.title: List Clipboard Formats
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard formats command lists MIME formats currently available from the live virtual machine clipboard.

listitem.para: Prints each MIME type as a separate format="..." line.

refsect2.title: Reset Clipboard State
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard reset command resets live clipboard data and transfer state for the virtual machine console.

refsect2.title: Serve Clipboard Data Between Guest and Host
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard serve command serves live clipboard updates between a running virtual machine and the native host clipboard. The command is a long-running API client: it listens for guest clipboard events and publishes supported guest-owned payloads to the &product-name; host clipboard endpoint. It uses a clipboard API session to suppress events caused by its own forwarding and conservative reflected updates, reducing host/guest echo loops. If only guest format information is available, it reports guest-owned formats and answers native host data requests by reading data through its clipboard API session. Host-origin format changes observed while serving are logged but not re-reported to the guest because the native host backend already reports them to the guest and Main. Native host clipboard backend logic remains in Main.

The shared clipboard mode must allow the requested transfer direction. Without a timeout, the command keeps serving clipboard events until it is interrupted. Pressing Ctrl+C stops the command cleanly after clearing the host clipboard endpoint and unregistering its event listener.

listitem.para: Stops serving after the specified number of milliseconds.
Prints diagnostic information to standard error. Repeating this option logs successfully copied payloads, bounded and escaped where possible.

refsect2.para: For example:
refsect2.screen: $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" serve --verbose $ VBoxManage clipboard "Ubuntu VM" serve -vv --timeout=30000

refsect2.title: Listen for Clipboard Events
refsect2.para: The VBoxManage clipboard listen command listens for live clipboard events from the virtual machine console. It prints event metadata by default, including revision and clientId fields when they are supplied by the clipboard API. The revision is a monotonic event revision for the live console clipboard. A clientId value of 0 means no IClipboardSession originated the event; a non-zero value identifies the originating clipboard API session. The command is observational by default; use VBoxManage clipboard copy to publish host clipboard data to the guest. With repeated -v or --verbose options, the command may read guest data for diagnostic output. Waitable clipboard events are acknowledged automatically before event output is written. Pressing Ctrl+C stops the command.

listitem.para: Specifies a comma-separated list of events to listen for. Valid values are source, format, data, data-requested, transfer, error, mode, and file-transfer-mode. The source-changed, format-changed, data-changed, and request aliases are also accepted for the corresponding events. The default is source,format,data,data-requested,transfer,error,mode,file-transfer-mode.

Specifies the event output format. JSON output is written as one JSON object per line. When event identity metadata is available, human output includes revision=<n> and clientId=<id>, machine-readable output includes revision="<n>" and clientId="<id>", and JSON output includes numeric revision and clientId members.
Stops listening after the specified number of milliseconds.
Stops listening after printing the specified number of events. A value of 0 means unlimited.
Shortcut for --format=machinereadable.
Increases verbosity. Repeating this option includes payload data in data-change output where available and may read guest data for diagnostics. Payload data is bounded and escaped where possible.
